objective: Add an initial Scholar specialty-agent scaffold inside packages/hldpro-sim for downstream Stampede consumption: packaged pointer/spec bundle, small loader/validator surface, bundled Scholar persona, and bounded consumer-proof tests.
out_of_scope: full canonical-source research completion and page-level citations
full fitness-test harness across canonical literature examples
new provider implementations or cloud API support
downstream product-repo adoption beyond bounded proof tests
research_summary: The current hldpro-sim package is persona-driven and generic: SimulationEngine, PersonaLoader, Runner, ArtifactWriter. A new specialty agent does not require a new provider/runner contract. The smallest real Scholar slice is a packaged capability surface plus one bundled Scholar persona and tests proving the capability can be loaded and invoked through the existing tooling. The operator-input runbook already fixes the five initial perspectives and the pointer-plus-spec architecture.

sprints.name: Sprint 1 — Scholar package surface
sprints.goal: Package the Scholar pointer/spec bundle and add a loader/validator surface.
tasks: bundle pointer.yaml and five perspective markdown files
acceptance_criteria: pointer file parses and resolves package-local perspective files
five committed perspectives are present and addressable by id
selected perspective requests return only requested specs

sprints.name: Sprint 2 — Scholar persona and tests
sprints.goal: Prove Scholar can be pulled through the existing simulation seams without new engine/provider behavior.
tasks: extend Stampede consumer proof with a Scholar-oriented invocation
acceptance_criteria: PersonaLoader resolves scholar-technical-analyst from package personas
bounded consumer proof passes through SimulationEngine, Runner, and ArtifactWriter
tests stay hermetic with mock providers only

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm Scholar fits the current hldpro-sim specialty-agent shape and does not require a new engine or provider abstraction.
specialist_reviews.summary: Current package is file-driven and persona-driven; a new capability can land as packaged data plus a small loader surface and bounded tests.

alternate_model_review.summary: This is a governance-owned package implementation slice, not a standards charter change.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement package-local Scholar scaffold, tests, and validation evidence only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Add scholar.py, package data, tests, and validation evidence; run bounded pytest commands and publish via PR.

material_deviation_rules: If implementation requires provider contract changes, HALT and open a follow-on issue.
If the five bundled perspective specs cannot remain bounded and package-local, HALT and split documentation research from runtime scaffold.
If tests require live model calls, HALT — this slice must remain hermetic.
